title: Fitting a hinge bolt to a door frame
subtitle: Fit a steel hinge bolt into the hinge side of an external door to prevent the door being forced by attacking the hinge pins
excerpt: How to fit hinge bolts to the hinge side of an exterior door. Covers positioning the bolts at hinge intervals, drilling the mortise into the door edge, fitting the receiver cup in the frame, and testing the engagement.
sourceNotes: UK locksmith and door-security trade practice. Insurance industry guidance on hinge-side security for external doors. Collins Complete DIY Manual (referenced, not reproduced).

glossaryTerms.definition: A security bolt fitted to the hinge side of an exterior door. When the door is closed, a steel peg (the bolt) protruding from the door edge drops into a matching cup in the frame. This prevents the hinge side of the door from being forced outward even if the hinge pins are knocked out (an attack method used on doors with exposed external hinge pins. Hinge bolts are passive (no key required) and engage automatically on closing. Two bolts are fitted between the door hinges.

content.text: locks the hinge side of an exterior door into the frame when the door is closed, protecting against attack on the hinge pins from outside. The approach is drilling two short mortise holes in the door edge, fitting the bolt body flush, and drilling a receiver cup in the frame that the bolt engages when the door closes.

Fit two hinge bolts between the hinges) typically 150 mm above the lower hinge and 150 mm below the upper hinge. Mark the bolt body position on the door edge, centred on the stile thickness. The bolt body sits flush with the door edge.
Drill and fit the bolt mortise

Drill the mortise into the door edge using the bit size specified for the bolt body (typically 16 to 20 mm) to the depth of the bolt body plus the bolt projection. Pare the mortise square and clean with a chisel. Press the bolt body into the mortise. The face of the bolt body should be flush with or very slightly below the door edge. Drive the retaining screw.
Mark and drill the frame receiver

Close the door gently. The bolt tip will mark the frame face. Open the door. Centre-punch the frame mark and drill the receiver hole to the depth the bolt protrudes when engaged (typically 12 to 15 mm. Fit the steel cup (the receiver ring) over the hole, flush with the frame face, and drive the fixing screws.

Close the door and check the bolt engages the receiver without snagging. Open the door) the bolt should spring back freely without sticking in the receiver. If the bolt is stiff to engage, widen the receiver cup hole by 1 mm.
